title: Full transcript: 23andMe CEO Anne Wojcicki answers genetics and privacy questions on Too Embarrassed to Ask | Vox
excerpts:
Section Title: Full transcript: 23andMe CEO Anne Wojcicki answers genetics and privacy questions on Too Embarras...
Content:
I think for us, part of what we’re trying to understand with all of this is how much of an influence do genetics have in each area. We don’t know. In some areas, it’s definitely going to have more of an influence than others, like brown eyes. You have certain genes, you are very likely to have brown eyes. Not 100 percent, but much more likely. That’s where environment’s not going to have as much of an influence. This is what we’re really trying to understand. Without a doubt, there’ll be all kinds of questions in the future.
**KS: Finally, before we get to questions, we have so many. Privacy, they wanted to talk about during this interview with you. Peoples’ genetic data goes in some kind of database. What happens when your company sells, goes public, is hacked, subpoenaed?**

title: 23andMe: How the DNA Pioneer Took On the FDA and Redefined Health Tech | Sequoia Capital
excerpts:
Section Title: 23andMe: How the DNA Pioneer Took On the FDA and Redefined Health Tech > ... > Richard Scheller
Content:
When Anne Wojcicki launched 23andMe in 2006, the company revolutionized the healthcare industry by becoming the first to offer individuals direct, affordable access to the information contained in their genome—information to help people understand their ancestry, but also their genetic predisposition to different health outcomes.
But as with any company that shakes up an industry—especially the medical establishment—there comes enormous risk and no guarantee of survival.
In today’s episode, we’ll hear how 23andMe confronted a regulator with the power to shut down the company, took a leap into the high-risk, high-reward territory of drug development, and transformed its consumer business after booming sales suddenly flatlined.
**Anne Wojcicki:** My name is Anne Wojcicki and I am the cofounder and CEO of 23andMe.

title: Anne Wojcicki, PDG de 23andMe : partenariat avec ...
Anne Wojcicki - InfluenceWatch - InfluenceWatch
excerpts:
Section Title: Anne Wojcicki > 23andMe
Content:
Anne Wojcicki co-founded the genetic testing company 23andMe in 2006 and has worked as the CEO since. 23andMe is an FDA-approved digital health company that allows customers to send in DNA samples through kits and from which the company derives information regarding their health based on their genetics. <sup>6</sup> As of March 2023, it had a $1.06 billion market cap, and as of November 2023 it had over 14 million customers. <sup>4</sup> <sup>7</sup>

title: Anne Wojcicki - Wikipedia
excerpts:
Section Title: Early life and education > Career > 23andMe
Content:
[edit ]
Wojcicki co-founded and was CEO of 23andMe , a direct-to-consumer DNA testing company that allowed consumers to analyze their ancestry and health risks. <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 11 ]](:1-11)</sup> She founded the company in 2006, with Linda Avey and Paul Cusenza, intending to provide common people access to their genetic information, which could potentially provide information on cures for diseases or treatments. <sup>[[ 11 ]](:1-11)</sup> <sup>[[ 12 ]]()</sup>
Consumers could purchase testing kits that provided information on ancestry, health, and genetic traits. The company took saliva samples that were mailed in by buyers, and processed the genetic data, posting the results online for the buyer to view. <sup>[[ 13 ]]()</sup>
